task_completion.description: Verify the internal client 'Executive Strategy Board' was created in Sentrifugo.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Sentrifugo 'Clients' or 'Time' module page showing 'Executive Strategy Board' in the list of clients, or a success message confirming its creation.

task_completion.description: Verify the project 'AI Enterprise Integration' was created and associated with the client.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Sentrifugo 'Projects' page showing 'AI Enterprise Integration' in the list, ideally showing its association with the 'Executive Strategy Board' client.

task_completion.description: Verify the project task 'Vendor Assessment' was created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'Vendor Assessment' in the Sentrifugo 'Project Tasks' list or within the project details configuration screen.

task_completion.description: Verify the project task 'Security Risk Analysis' was created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'Security Risk Analysis' in the Sentrifugo 'Project Tasks' list or within the project details configuration screen.

task_completion.description: Verify the project task 'Pilot Implementation' was created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'Pilot Implementation' in the Sentrifugo 'Project Tasks' list or within the project details configuration screen.

task_completion.description: Verify the three specified employees (EMP006, EMP012, EMP015) were allocated to the project.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Sentrifugo resource allocation or project members page showing the employee IDs EMP006, EMP012, and EMP015 (or the name Kevin Hernandez for EMP015) assigned to the 'AI Enterprise Integration' project.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Sentrifugo web interface to configure the Time module.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent navigating the Sentrifugo web UI in a browser, rather than executing backend database scripts or API curl commands in a terminal.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually entered the data into the Sentrifugo forms.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show form fields being actively filled out or success messages generated by the Sentrifugo UI, rather than just a text file with the answers.

integrity.description: Verify the agent opened and read the charter document to get the requirements.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the file 'taskforce_charter.txt' open in a text editor, cat/less command in the terminal, or similar file viewer at some point during the trajectory.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected client name is 'Executive Strategy Board'. The expected project name is 'AI Enterprise Integration'. The three expected project tasks are 'Vendor Assessment', 'Security Risk Analysis', and 'Pilot Implementation'. The expected employee allocations are EMP006, EMP012, and EMP015. Note: EMP015 is named 'Kevin Hernandez' in the system, even though the charter document erroneously refers to him as 'Kevin Robinson'. The VLM should look for the employee ID EMP015 or the name Kevin Hernandez when verifying the third employee allocation.
